topic: CrewAI Tutorial 2026: Build Multi-Agent Systems in Python Step by Step

competitors.title: How to Build Multi-Agent AI with CrewAI Python in 13 Steps [2026]
key_points: CrewAI has become the fastest-growing multi-agent AI framework in 2026 with over 14,800 monthly searches
Provides 13-step tutorial from installation to production deployment
Uses YAML-based configuration for agents and tasks (agents.yaml, tasks.yaml)
Integrates with multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Ollama) via LiteLLM
Includes built-in tools (search, scrape, file, PDF) through crewai[tools] package
Supports structured outputs with Pydantic models for reliable data pipelines
Implements memory layers: short-term, long-term, and entity memory for agent persistence
Offers Crews for single workflows and Flows for multi-stage orchestration
Includes production deployment patterns with FastAPI and error handling
Compares CrewAI vs LangGraph vs AutoGen with feature matrix

competitors.title: GitHub - crewAIInc/crewAI: Framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents
key_points: Framework built from scratch independent of LangChain or other frameworks
49k stars on GitHub, 6.7k forks (as of April 2026)
Supports Crews (collaborative agent teams) and Flows (multi-stage orchestration)
Open-source MIT license with complete documentation at docs.crewai.com

stats.fact: CrewAI has over 14,800 monthly searches in 2026, fastest-growing multi-agent framework
GitHub repository has 49k stars and 6.7k forks as of April 2026
Version 1.14.2rc1 released April 15, 2026 with 2,277 total commits
Supports multiple LLM providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Azure OpenAI, Ollama

angles: Role-based agent paradigm vs graph-based alternatives (LangGraph)
Production deployment patterns for multi-agent systems
Cost optimization strategies for multi-LLM agent systems
Structured outputs and memory management for reliable agent pipelines
Comparison framework: CrewAI vs LangGraph vs AutoGen for different use cases
outline: Introduction: The Rise of Multi-Agent AI Frameworks in 2026
Why CrewAI? Fastest-Growing Framework for Agent Collaboration
Prerequisites: Python Environment and LLM API Setup
Step 1: Installing CrewAI and Creating Your First Project
Step 2: Configuring LLM Providers with Environment Variables
Step 3: Defining Agents in YAML with Roles, Goals, and Backstories

Step 5: Building Crew Definition with Python Decorators
Step量与6: Running the Crew and Monitoring Execution
Step 7: Adding Tools for Web Search, Scraping, and File Operations
Step 8: Creating Custom Tools for Business-Specific Operations
Step 9: Implementing Structured Outputs with Pydantic Models
Step 10: Using CrewAI Flows for Complex Orchestration
Step 11: Adding Memory and Context for Agent Persistence
Step 12: Error Handling, Guardrails, and Production Considerations
Step 13: Deploying to Production with FastAPI and Monitoring
Comparison: CrewAI vs LangGraph vs AutoGen Feature Matrix
Troubleshooting Common Issues with CrewAI Agents
Advanced Tips for Production CrewAI Systems
Conclusion: Building Production-Ready Multi-Agent Systems
